final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing panoramic artwork of the Tang Dynasty Chang'an Lantern Festival, with thousands of lanterns illuminating the night sky, intended for editorial illustration or cultural heritage display.

Style/medium: meticulous Chinese gongbi painting in the tradition of Tang Dynasty court scrolls, with the patient accumulation of detail found in historical narrative handscrolls
Composition: continuous horizontal handscroll format, eye traveling through the festival scene as a journey from city gate through palace district to common streets
Subject: thousands of Tang Dynasty lanterns of varied shapes—lotus, phoenix, dragon, celestial orbs—suspended across streets and palace courtyards, crowds in traditional dress admiring the spectacle
Scene/backdrop: Tang Dynasty Chang'an cityscape at night, palaces with upturned eaves, pagodas, market streets, the Big Wild Goose Pagoda visible in distance, all under a vast ink-wash sky
Lighting/mood: the sacred warmth of ten thousand flames against indigo darkness, a collective breath held in wonder, the rhythm of light echoing through ancient streets
Color palette: mineral pigments in cinnabar red, azurite blue, malachite green, and gold leaf accents, the temperature of firelight dancing against cool night ink
Materials/textures: silk scroll ground with the layered transparency of mineral pigments, fine brushwork defining architectural detail and the soft glow of lantern paper
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: Tang Dynasty architectural accuracy, thousands of lanterns visible, continuous scroll format
Avoid: anachronistic elements, flat digital vector aesthetic, Western perspective, generic festival stock imagery
